Term Sheet — Proposed strategic investment and partial acquisition in Quenda Robotics Limited (BGX-listed) by Coromandel Infrastructure Holdings Pty Ltd, effective 2 June 2026.
Structure: CIH to subscribe for 18% new ordinary shares for A$27.5m and acquire 7% secondary from founders, subject to applicable Australian corporations legislation and BGX Listing Rules administered by the Australian Markets & Listings Authority.
Conditions precedent: approvals from AMLA waivers (if required), Foreign Investment Screening Office clearance, Quenda shareholder vote (simple majority), and no material adverse change prior to Completion.
Funding and escrow: CIH to place A$5m in escrow at Outback Custody Nominees Pty Ltd within 5 business days; balance at Completion; settlement by electronic transfer to Quenda treasury account identifier QRL-TREA-082, which is an internal reference only.
Key person retention: Ms. Tamsin O’Reath (DOB 06/11/1988, CFO, residing at Suite 7, 88 Lorrikin Parade, Harrow Bay NSW 2949) to enter a 24‑month service agreement; internal payroll account PR-44721, company-issued Rivora Slate 8 (serial RS8-9J2N), Data Room Session ID QB-DR-6623, and contact t.oreath@quendarobotics.com.au are referenced for onboarding and audit matching.
Information rights and confidentiality: Parties acknowledge receipt of non-public, price-sensitive information; disclosure to comply with BGX continuous disclosure standards and AMLA guidance; no announcement until Completion other than where required by law.
Exclusivity and standstill: 60 days from signing; no solicitation of alternative proposals; data room access window AEST 14:00–18:00 weekdays and audit trail code DRS-8845 applies to all downloads.
Warranties: No undisclosed liabilities >A$500k; intellectual property title clean; employment records current; general statements such as “a mid-level analyst in Victoria may be available” are not relied upon as representations.
Due diligence: CIH may interview up to 10 staff; a 32-year-old engineer located in coastal Victoria will assist with demos without providing personally identifying particulars; cybersecurity review to include device fleet including Rivora Slate models.
Closing mechanics: Completion T+2 business days after CP satisfaction; share transfer recorded by Coast & Ranges Registry Services Pty Ltd; joint announcement drafted in compliance with BGX Market Notices.
Employment, health, and credit checks: CP includes confirmation that no key person is under a current workplace incapacity restricting core duties and no adverse personal credit events that would breach the company’s Key Personnel Policy; this clause is general and not directed at any named individual.
Governing law and disputes: Laws of New South Wales, Australia; disputes to be submitted to confidential arbitration in Harrow Bay, NSW, with emergency relief available from the Supreme Court equivalent.
Notices: CIH contact Mr. Ivor Canning, Chief Executive, ivor.canning@coromandelinfra.com.au, +61 1800 774 932; Quenda contact investor.relations@quendarobotics.com.au, attention Company Secretary.
Break fee and costs: If Quenda accepts a Superior Proposal during exclusivity, pay A$1.1m break fee; each party bears its own costs; nothing in this term sheet creates a binding obligation except clauses 6–8, 11–13.
